articles.Title: Getting Started with the Kinetic Platform
articles.Content:
The Kinetic Platform is a modern service management platform that enables organizations to automate workflows, build custom applications, and manage service delivery. This guide covers the basics of navigation, creating your first form, and submitting requests through the self-service portal.

## Key Concepts
- **Kapps**: Application containers that group related forms and workflows
- **Forms**: Data collection interfaces with configurable fields and validation
- **Workflows**: Automated processes triggered by form submissions
- **Submissions**: Individual records created when users complete forms
articles.Author: Sarah Chen

articles.Title: How to Reset Your Password
articles.Content:
If you've forgotten your password or need to reset it for security reasons, follow these steps:

1. Navigate to the login page
2. Click 'Forgot Password' below the login form
3. Enter your registered email address
4. Check your email for the reset link (valid for 15 minutes)
5. Click the link and enter your new password
6. Password must be at least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

**Note:** If you don't receive the email within 5 minutes, check your spam folder or contact the IT Help Desk.
articles.Author: Mark Thompson

articles.Title: VPN Setup Guide for Remote Workers
articles.Content:
This guide covers setting up VPN access for working remotely.

## Prerequisites
- Company-issued laptop with latest OS updates
- Multi-factor authentication configured
- Network access approved by your manager

## Installation
1. Download GlobalProtect from the IT Software Portal
2. Install with default settings
3. Enter gateway: vpn.company.com
4. Log in with your corporate credentials
5. Approve the MFA prompt on your authenticator app

## Troubleshooting
- If connection drops frequently, try switching to TCP mode in Settings
- For split-tunnel issues, contact Network Engineering
articles.Author: Sarah Chen

articles.Title: Submitting a Service Request
articles.Content:
Service requests are used to ask for something new, such as access to a system, new equipment, or a software license.

## How to Submit
1. Log in to the Self-Service Portal
2. Navigate to the Service Catalog
3. Browse categories or search for what you need
4. Fill in the request form with all required details
5. Add business justification where required
6. Submit the request

## What Happens Next
- Your request enters an approval workflow
- Your manager receives a notification for approval
- Once approved, the fulfillment team is assigned
- You'll receive email updates at each stage
- Average fulfillment time: 2-3 business days
articles.Author: Emily Watson

articles.Title: Email Encryption Best Practices
articles.Content:
When sending sensitive information via email, follow these encryption guidelines:

## When to Encrypt
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII)
- Financial data or account numbers
- Health-related information
- Confidential business documents
- Legal communications

## How to Encrypt in Outlook
1. Compose your email
2. Click Options > Encrypt
3. Select the appropriate encryption level
4. Recipients will need to verify their identity to read the message

## External Recipients
External recipients receive a link to a secure portal where they authenticate before viewing the encrypted message.
articles.Author: Mark Thompson

articles.Title: Setting Up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
articles.Content:
Multi-factor authentication adds a second layer of security to your account. It is required for all employees.

## Setup Steps
1. Download Microsoft Authenticator from your app store
2. Log in to myaccount.microsoft.com
3. Navigate to Security Info > Add method
4. Select Authenticator App and follow the prompts
5. Scan the QR code with the Authenticator app
6. Enter the verification code to confirm

## Backup Methods
- Add a phone number as a backup for SMS codes
- Print recovery codes and store securely
- Register a second device if available
articles.Author: Mark Thompson

articles.Title: Configuring Outlook Calendar Sharing
articles.Content:
Share your calendar with colleagues to improve scheduling and collaboration.

## Share Your Calendar
1. Open Outlook Calendar
2. Right-click your calendar name
3. Select Sharing Permissions
4. Add the person's email address
5. Set the permission level (Free/Busy, Limited Details, Full Details, Editor)
6. Click Send

## Best Practices
- Share at least Free/Busy with your team
- Use categories to color-code meeting types
- Keep your calendar up to date for accurate availability
- Block focus time to protect deep work periods
articles.Author: Emily Watson

articles.Title: Requesting Access to Cloud Services
articles.Content:
Access to AWS, Azure, and GCP environments requires approval through the Cloud Access Request process.

## Steps
1. Submit a Cloud Access Request through the Service Catalog
2. Specify the cloud provider, account, and role needed
3. Include business justification and project name
4. Your manager and the Cloud Team must both approve
5. Access is provisioned within 24 hours of final approval

## Important Notes
- Access is reviewed quarterly
- Unused access is automatically revoked after 90 days
- Production access requires additional security training completion
articles.Author: David Kim
articles.Tags: cloud, aws, azure, gcp, access, provisioning

articles.Title: Incident Severity Classification Guide
articles.Content:
Use this guide to determine the correct severity when reporting an incident.

## Severity Levels

| Severity | Criteria | Response Time | Examples |
|----------|----------|---------------|----------|
| Critical | Business-wide outage | 15 min | Email server down, security breach |
| High | Department-level impact | 1 hour | Application unavailable, data loss risk |
| Medium | Individual impact with workaround | 4 hours | Slow performance, minor feature broken |
| Low | Cosmetic or informational | 8 hours | Typo in UI, feature request |

## When in Doubt
Choose the higher severity. The support team can adjust after assessment.
articles.Author: Sarah Chen

articles.Title: New Employee IT Onboarding Checklist
articles.Content:
Welcome to the team! Complete these IT setup tasks during your first week.

## Day 1
- [ ] Activate your company account (check email from IT)
- [ ] Set up MFA (see KB-006)
- [ ] Connect to WiFi: network 'CorpNet', use your credentials
- [ ] Configure VPN if working remotely (see KB-003)

## Day 2-3
- [ ] Set up Outlook email and calendar
- [ ] Join required Slack channels
- [ ] Access the Self-Service Portal
- [ ] Install required software from IT Portal

## Day 4-5
- [ ] Complete Security Awareness Training
- [ ] Verify access to team-specific applications
- [ ] Schedule intro call with your IT buddy
articles.Author: Mike Torres
articles.Tags: onboarding, new hire, checklist, setup, first day

articles.Title: Kubernetes Deployment Best Practices
articles.Content:
Follow these guidelines when deploying applications to our Kubernetes clusters.

## Resource Management
- Always set CPU and memory requests and limits
- Use Horizontal Pod Autoscalers for variable workloads
- Set pod disruption budgets for high-availability services

## Security
- Run containers as non-root
- Use network policies to restrict pod-to-pod communication
- Scan images with Trivy before deployment
- Store secrets in Vault, not Kubernetes secrets

## Monitoring
- Implement readiness and liveness probes
- Export Prometheus metrics
- Set up alerts for error rates and latency
articles.Author: David Kim
articles.Tags: kubernetes, deployment, containers, devops, best practices

articles.Title: Printer Troubleshooting Common Issues
articles.Content:
Try these steps before contacting the help desk about printer problems.

## Printer Offline
1. Check the printer is powered on
2. Verify the network cable is connected
3. Restart the print spooler service
4. Remove and re-add the printer

## Paper Jams
1. Open all access panels
2. Gently remove jammed paper (pull in the direction of paper path)
3. Check for torn pieces
4. Close panels and run a test print

## Poor Print Quality
- Run the cleaning cycle from the printer menu
- Check toner/ink levels
- Align print heads if colors are offset
articles.Author: Mike Torres
articles.Tags: printer, troubleshooting, hardware, paper jam, offline

articles.Title: Data Classification and Handling Policy
articles.Content:
All company data must be classified and handled according to its sensitivity level.

## Classification Levels
- **Public**: Marketing materials, job postings
- **Internal**: Team docs, meeting notes, project plans
- **Confidential**: Financial reports, customer data, HR records
- **Restricted**: Trade secrets, M&A data, security credentials

## Handling Requirements
- Public: No restrictions
- Internal: Company network only, no external sharing without approval
- Confidential: Encrypted storage and transmission, access logging
- Restricted: Need-to-know access, DLP controls, no cloud storage
articles.Author: Mark Thompson
articles.Tags: data classification, security, compliance, policy, handling

articles.Title: Using Microsoft Teams Effectively
articles.Content:
Get the most out of Microsoft Teams with these tips.

## Channels vs Chat
- Use **channels** for team discussions that others should be able to find later
- Use **chat** for quick 1:1 or small group conversations
- Pin important messages in channels

## Meetings
- Always include an agenda in the meeting invite
- Record meetings that include decisions
- Use breakout rooms for workshops
- Enable live captions for accessibility

## Files
- Store shared files in the channel Files tab (backed by SharePoint)
- Co-author documents in real-time
- Use @mentions to notify specific people
articles.Author: Emily Watson

articles.Title: WiFi Connectivity Troubleshooting
articles.Content:
Follow these steps if you are having WiFi connectivity issues in the office.

## Basic Steps
1. Toggle WiFi off and on
2. Forget the network and reconnect
3. Restart your laptop
4. Move closer to an access point

## Advanced Steps
1. Run network diagnostics (Windows: Settings > Network > Troubleshoot)
2. Flush DNS cache: ipconfig /flushdns
3. Reset TCP/IP stack: netsh int ip reset
4. Check if the issue is location-specific

## Known Dead Zones
- Building B lobby (ticket open for additional AP)
- Parking garage level 2
- Building A stairwells
articles.Author: Sarah Chen

articles.Title: Backup and Recovery Procedures
articles.Content:
Understanding how your data is backed up and how to request a recovery.

## What's Backed Up
- OneDrive files: continuous sync + daily snapshots, 93-day retention
- SharePoint sites: daily backups, 14-day retention
- Email: continuous journaling, 7-year retention
- Databases: hourly snapshots, 30-day retention

## Requesting a Restore
1. Submit an incident with category 'Data Recovery'
2. Specify the file/folder path and approximate date of the good copy
3. Recovery SLA: 4 hours for standard, 1 hour for critical

## Self-Service Recovery
- OneDrive: Version History (right-click file > Version History)
- SharePoint: Recycle Bin (up to 93 days)
articles.Author: David Kim
articles.Tags: backup, recovery, restore, data protection, onedrive

articles.Title: Phishing Email Identification Guide
articles.Content:
Learn to recognize and report phishing emails to protect our organization.

## Red Flags
- Sender address doesn't match the displayed name
- Urgent language: 'Act now', 'Account suspended', 'Verify immediately'
- Suspicious links (hover to check before clicking)
- Unexpected attachments, especially .exe, .zip, or .docm
- Generic greeting instead of your name
- Grammar and spelling errors

## What To Do
1. Do NOT click any links or download attachments
2. Use the 'Report Phishing' button in Outlook
3. If you already clicked, disconnect from network and call Security (ext. 5555)
4. Change your password immediately if you entered credentials
articles.Author: Mark Thompson
articles.Tags: phishing, email, security, awareness, social engineering

articles.Title: API Gateway Configuration Standards
articles.Content:
Standards and guidelines for configuring API gateways in our environment.

## Authentication
- All APIs must use OAuth 2.0 or JWT bearer tokens
- API keys are acceptable only for internal service-to-service calls
- mTLS required for production APIs handling sensitive data

## Rate Limiting
- Default: 100 requests/second per client
- Burst: 200 requests/second (5-second window)
- Custom limits require Architecture Review Board approval

## Monitoring
- Enable request/response logging (mask sensitive fields)
- Set up alerts for 5xx error rate > 1%
- Dashboard: Grafana > API Gateway panel
articles.Author: David Kim

articles.Title: How to Request a Hardware Repair
articles.Content:
If your company-issued hardware needs repair, follow this process.

## Covered Repairs
- Laptop screen replacement
- Keyboard/trackpad issues
- Battery replacement
- Docking station problems
- Monitor failures

## Process
1. Submit an incident through the Self-Service Portal
2. Category: Hardware, describe the issue
3. IT will assess and provide a loaner if needed
4. Repair turnaround: 3-5 business days for in-warranty, 5-10 for out-of-warranty

## Important
- Back up your data before submitting the device
- Remove any personal items from laptop bags
- Keep your ticket number for tracking
articles.Author: Mike Torres

articles.Title: Change Management Process Overview
articles.Content:
All changes to production systems must follow the change management process.

## Change Types
- **Standard**: Pre-approved, low risk (e.g., adding firewall rule)
- **Normal**: Requires CAB approval, scheduled maintenance window
- **Emergency**: Critical fix, expedited approval from CISO or VP

## Process
1. Create a Change Request with description and risk assessment
2. Attach test results and rollback plan
3. Submit for approval
4. CAB reviews Normal changes every Wednesday
5. Implement during approved window
6. Verify and close the change

## Lead Times
- Standard: 2 business days
- Normal: 5 business days (for CAB review)
- Emergency: Immediate (post-implementation review required)
articles.Author: Sarah Chen
articles.Tags: change management, process, cab, itil, governance
